question_text: A satellite orbits Earth because gravity pulls it towards Earth, but it also has forward motion. What does this combination create?

options.label: A
options.text: A straight line away from Earth

options.label: C
options.text: A spiral path into Earth's atmosphere

options.label: D
options.text: A parabolic trajectory that escapes Earth

explanation: Gravity constantly pulls the satellite towards Earth (downward), while its forward motion tries to move it in a straight line. These two forces balance to create a stable circular or elliptical orbit around Earth.
